records.ety: [OE. stirop, AS. stigrāp; stīgan to mount, ascend + rāp a rope; akin to G. stegreif a stirrup. √164. See Sty, v. i., and Rope.]

defs: A kind of ring, or bent piece of metal, wood, leather, or the like, horizontal in one part for receiving the foot of a rider, and attached by a strap to the saddle, -- used to assist a person in mounting a horse, and to enable him to sit steadily in riding, as well as to relieve him by supporting a part of the weight of the body.
quotes: Our host upon his stirpoes stood anon.

defs: Any piece resembling in shape the stirrup of a saddle, and used as a support, clamp, etc. See Bridle iron.
A rope secured to a yard, with a thimble in its lower end for supporting a footrope.
